```{r with interactions}
         p.value = as.numeric(p.value)) 

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# Create a kable table
model_sumfull_clean %>%
    kable(format = "html", digits = 3, col.names = c("Group", "Predictor", "Estimate", "Standard Error", "t Value", "p Value")) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive")) %>%
    add_header_above(c("Model Summary" = 6)) %>%
    add_footnote("Model summary for each group (ECEO = 0, 1)")

model_sumorig_clean %>%
    kable(format = "html", digits = 3, col.names = c("Group", "Predictor", "Estimate", "Standard Error", "t Value", "p Value")) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive")) %>%
    add_header_above(c("Model Summary" = 6)) %>%
    add_footnote("Model summary for each group (ECEO = 0, 1)")
#sig for post tb ratio; pre tb ratio predicts post tb ratio


#using PCIT binary
modelfull_binary <- caps_hr %>% 
    group_by(ECEO) %>% 
    nest() %>% 
    mutate(model = map(data, ~ {
        if (nrow(.x) > 0) {
            lm(trans_tb_post ~ trans_tb_pre * PCIT_binary * age * ACE * CHAOS, data = .x)
        } else {
            NA  
        }
    }))

# Filter out the NULL models
modelfull_binary <- modelfull_binary %>% filter(!is.null(model))

# Extract and print the summaries of each model
model_sumfull_binary <- modelfull_binary %>%
  mutate(summary = map(model, tidy)) %>%
  select(ECEO, summary) %>%
  unnest(summary)

print(model_sumfull_binary)

modelsfullorig_binary <- caps_hr %>%
  group_by(ECEO) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(model = map(data, ~ if(nrow(.x) > 0) lm(tb_post_trans_orig ~ tb_pre_trans_orig * PCIT_binary * age * ACE * CHAOS, data = .x) else NULL))

# Filter out the NULL models
modelsfullorig_binary <- modelsfullorig_binary %>% filter(!is.null(model))

# Extract and print the summaries of each model
model_sumorig_binary <- modelsfullorig_binary %>%
  mutate(summary = map(model, tidy)) %>%
  select(ECEO, summary) %>%
  unnest(summary)

print(model_sumfull_binary)
print(model_sumorig_binary)

model_sumfull_clean_binary <- model_sumfull_binary %>%
  select(ECEO, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value) %>%
  mutate(ECEO = as.factor(ECEO),
         estimate = as.numeric(estimate),
         std.error = as.numeric(std.error),
         statistic = as.numeric(statistic),
         p.value = as.numeric(p.value)) 

model_sumorig_clean_binary <- model_sumorig_binary %>%
  select(ECEO, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value) %>%
  mutate(ECEO = as.factor(ECEO),
         estimate = as.numeric(estimate),
         std.error = as.numeric(std.error),
         statistic = as.numeric(statistic),
         p.value = as.numeric(p.value)) 

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# Create a kable table
model_sumfull_clean_binary %>%
    kable(format = "html", digits = 3, col.names = c("Group", "Predictor", "Estimate", "Standard Error", "t Value", "p Value")) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive")) %>%
    add_header_above(c("Model Summary" = 6)) %>%
    add_footnote("Model summary for each group (ECEO = 0, 1)")

model_sumorig_clean_binary %>%
    kable(format = "html", digits = 3, col.names = c("Group", "Predictor", "Estimate", "Standard Error", "t Value", "p Value")) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive")) %>%
    add_header_above(c("Model Summary" = 6)) %>%
    add_footnote("Model summary for each group (ECEO = 0, 1)")
```

```{r bias measurement}
caps_rest <- caps_rest %>%
    mutate(
        bias_pre  = trans_tb_pre  - tb_pre_trans_orig,
        bias_post = trans_tb_post - tb_post_trans_orig,
        condition = if_else(str_detect(filename1, "EC"), "EC", "EO")
    )

# Step 2: get all combinations of condition and PCIT
combinations <- caps_rest %>% distinct(condition, PCIT)

# Step 3: helper to compute Cohen's d for a vector
compute_d <- function(x, timepoint, cond, pcit) {
    d <- cohens_d(x, mu = 0, ci = 0.95)
    as.data.frame(d) %>% mutate(timepoint = timepoint, condition = cond, PCIT = pcit)
}

# Step 4: loop over combinations and timepoints
results_list <- list()

for(i in 1:nrow(combinations)) {
    cond <- combinations$condition[i]
    pcit <- combinations$PCIT[i]
    
    subset_data <- filter(caps_rest, condition == cond, PCIT == pcit)
    
    # Pre
    results_list[[length(results_list) + 1]] <- compute_d(subset_data$bias_pre, "pre", cond, pcit)
    
    # Post
    results_list[[length(results_list) + 1]] <- compute_d(subset_data$bias_post, "post", cond, pcit)
}

# Step 5: combine all into one table
results <- bind_rows(results_list) %>%
    arrange(PCIT, timepoint, condition)

results

#for only HR people
only_hr<-caps_rest %>%
  group_by(filename1)%>%
  filter(heartrate_pre==1|heartrate_post==1)%>%
  pull(filename1) %>%
  unique()

only_hr <- caps_rest %>%
  filter(filename1 %in% only_hr)

only_hr <- only_hr %>%
    mutate(
        bias_pre  = trans_tb_pre  - tb_pre_trans_orig,
        bias_post = trans_tb_post - tb_post_trans_orig,
        condition = if_else(str_detect(filename1, "EC"), "EC", "EO")
    )

# Step 2: get all combinations of condition and PCIT
combinations <- only_hr %>% distinct(condition, PCIT)

# Step 3: helper to compute Cohen's d for a vector
compute_d <- function(x, timepoint, cond, pcit) {
    d <- cohens_d(x, mu = 0, ci = 0.95)
    as.data.frame(d) %>% mutate(timepoint = timepoint, condition = cond, PCIT = pcit)
}

# Step 4: loop over combinations and timepoints
results_list <- list()

for(i in 1:nrow(combinations)) {
    cond <- combinations$condition[i]
    pcit <- combinations$PCIT[i]
    
    subset_data <- filter(only_hr, condition == cond, PCIT == pcit)
    
    # Pre
    results_list[[length(results_list) + 1]] <- compute_d(subset_data$bias_pre, "pre", cond, pcit)
    
    # Post
    results_list[[length(results_list) + 1]] <- compute_d(subset_data$bias_post, "post", cond, pcit)
}

# Step 5: combine all into one table
results_hr <- bind_rows(results_list) %>%
    arrange(PCIT, timepoint, condition)

results_hr


#t-test
comparison_data <- results %>%
  select(condition, PCIT, timepoint, Cohens_d) %>%
  rename(cohens_d_full = Cohens_d) %>%
  left_join(
    results_hr %>% select(condition, PCIT, timepoint, Cohens_d) %>%
    rename(cohens_d_hr = Cohens_d),
    by = c("condition", "PCIT", "timepoint")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(cohens_d_hr)) # Remove cases where HR data doesn't exist

# Check the merged data
print("Comparison data:")
print(comparison_data)
print(paste("Number of paired observations:", nrow(comparison_data)))

# Paired t-test
t_test_result <- t.test(
  comparison_data$cohens_d_hr, 
  comparison_data$cohens_d_full,
  paired = TRUE  # Testing if HR > Full
)

print(t_test_result)

```

```{r iclabel %}
df <- data.frame(
  Other = 27,
  Brain = 13,
  Muscle = 1,
  Eye = 24,
  Heart = 0,
  `Line Noise` = 0,
  `Channel Noise` = 4
)

df

library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# Your data
df <- data.frame(
  Other = 27,
  Brain = 13,
  Muscle = 1,
  Eye = 24,
  Heart = 0,
  `Line Noise` = 0,
  `Channel Noise` = 4
)

# Convert to long format
df_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), 
               names_to = "Channel", 
               values_to = "Count")

# Bar graph
ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Channel, y = Count, fill = Channel)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +  # Choose a palette like Set1, Set2, Set3
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Highest ICLABEL %", y = "Count", x = "ICLABEL Categories") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1)) +
  guides(fill = FALSE) +
 theme(
  axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
  axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
  plot.title = element_text(size = 16),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
  legend.title = element_text(size = 13)
)
# optional: remove legend

```
